V19a.name: Read a whole loom file into a program
V19a.blurb: Parses an entire .loom file — every statement, not just one query — into a structured program the runner can execute.

V19b.name: Track variables and scope while running
V19b.blurb: Provides the runtime name-and-scope model — variables, mutability, loop bindings, function lookup — the runner evaluates against.

V19c.name: Run a loom program step by step
V19c.blurb: Walks the parsed program top to bottom — bindings, loops, branches, matches — producing the final value, with clean cancellation points.

V19d.name: Connect running programs to real effects
V19d.blurb: Wires the runner to real model queries, tool calls, and nested loom invocations so a program's actions actually happen.

V19e.name: Turn a loom file into a working command
V19e.blurb: Assembles parsing, argument-binding, and running into the one step that makes a discovered loom file an executable slash command.

H1a.name: Set up the project skeleton
H1a.blurb: Creates the base project, dependencies, and build and test commands everything else is built on.

H2a.name: Code-quality safety checks
H2a.blurb: Automatic checks that block risky coding patterns before they reach the project.

H3a.name: Wire up swappable host services
H3a.blurb: Sets up replaceable connections to host services so nothing reaches global state directly.

H4a.name: Extension start-up and test harness
H4a.blurb: Starts the add-on inside the host and provides a test rig for end-to-end runs.

H4b.name: Scriptable fake model for tests
H4b.blurb: Lets tests script what the stand-in model replies, so behaviour can be checked reliably.

H4c.name: Scriptable nested calls and sub-assistants
H4c.blurb: Extends the test stand-in to script completed nested calls and separate sub-assistant runs.

H5a.name: Coverage gate for requirements
H5a.blurb: Automatically checks every requirement and message code has a matching test.

H5b.name: Pre-release coverage warning pass
H5b.blurb: Warns about coverage gaps before the strict release check turns them into failures.

H5c.name: Audit error-catching exemptions
H5c.blurb: Checks that every broad error-catch exemption points to a real, valid reason.

H5d.name: Check every requirement has an owner
H5d.blurb: Confirms each requirement is assigned to at least one piece of planned work.

H5e.name: Find unlabelled must-do rules
H5e.blurb: Scans the spec for mandatory rules that lack a tracked owner or test.

H5f.name: Check shared requirements are fully tested
H5f.blurb: Ensures each part of a requirement split across tasks has its own test.

H6a.name: Turn on the release coverage gate
H6a.blurb: Flips coverage checks to the real project at release time, after smoke and review.

H7a.name: End-to-end integration check
H7a.blurb: Runs one rich example through the whole pipeline to catch cross-feature regressions.

H8a.name: Live end-to-end product check
H8a.blurb: Actually launches the shipped add-on against a real model: a loom file on disk becomes a working slash command that runs a live turn — the composition unit tests can't see.

M.name: Run a basic loom command
M.blurb: The smallest working command: read a file, ask the model once, and stream the reply.

V1a.name: Read and tokenise loom source
V1a.blurb: Turns raw loom text into the tokens the rest of the language works with.

V1b.name: Read text, number, and path values
V1b.blurb: Reads quoted strings, numbers, and file paths written directly in loom source.

V2a.name: Read type and value notation
V2a.blurb: Parses the notation for declaring types and writing literal values in loom.

V2b.name: Check type compatibility
V2b.blurb: Decides whether one type fits where another is expected, with clear mismatch messages.

V2c.name: Represent values at runtime
V2c.blurb: Defines how loom values are held in memory and compared for equality.

V2d.name: Format numbers consistently
V2d.blurb: Renders whole numbers and decimals in one consistent text form across the system.

V2e.name: Translate field names on the wire
V2e.blurb: Converts field names between loom-side and external JSON forms in both directions.

V3a.name: Evaluate expressions
V3a.blurb: Computes expressions: maths, comparisons, logic, and building objects and arrays.

V3b.name: Declare and reassign variables
V3b.blurb: Adds fixed and changeable variables with rules on what may be reassigned.

V3c.name: Branch and loop
V3c.blurb: Adds if, for, while, break, and continue for branching and repetition.

V3d.name: Define and return from functions
V3d.blurb: Adds top-level functions with inferred return types and return rules.

V3f.name: Built-in text helpers
V3f.blurb: Adds common text operations like split, trim, change case, and contains.

V3g.name: Built-in list helpers
V3g.blurb: Adds common list operations like join, slice, includes, and length.

V3h.name: Built-in object helpers
V3h.blurb: Adds object helpers to read keys, read values, and check for a field.

V4a.name: Pattern matching and results
V4a.blurb: Adds match, the success-or-error result type, and the shortcut error operator.

V4b.name: Handle unrecoverable runtime errors
V4b.blurb: Defines the fixed set of fatal runtime errors and their standard messages.

V4c.name: Define success, failure, cancelled
V4c.blurb: Classifies every run as success, failure, or cancelled, keeping work done so far.

V4d.name: Define question-failure shapes
V4d.blurb: Defines the set of error shapes a model question can fail with.

V4e.name: Report load-time problems
V4e.blurb: Surfaces problems found before running as notes, not as run outcomes.

V4f.name: Keep completed work on failure
V4f.blurb: Guarantees finished side effects survive a later failure; nothing is undone.

V4g.name: Report reload-time problems
V4g.blurb: Surfaces problems found while reloading changed files as notes, not run outcomes.

V5a.name: Declare data schemas
V5a.blurb: Adds declarations for object, alias, and enum data shapes with named fields.

V5b.name: Either-or and recursive schemas
V5b.blurb: Supports either-or data shapes, self-referencing schemas, and cycle detection.

V5c.name: Attach descriptions to schemas
V5c.blurb: Lets doc comments become field descriptions carried into the schema.

V5d.name: Reject unsupported schema features
V5d.blurb: Accepts only the supported schema subset and clearly rejects everything else.

V5e.name: Limit data nesting depth
V5e.blurb: Rejects data nested deeper than the safety limit before validation runs.

V5f.name: Finalise and fingerprint schemas
V5f.blurb: Converts schemas to final form and computes a stable identifying fingerprint.

V6a.name: Read prompt-file settings
V6a.blurb: Parses the settings block at the top of a loom file, with defaults and warnings.

V6b.name: Declare command parameters
V6b.blurb: Defines a command's parameters, their types, and default values.

V6c.name: Declare which tools a command uses
V6c.blurb: Defines the set of tools a command may call, with renaming and clash checks.

V6d.name: Fill in the system-prompt template
V6d.blurb: Builds a sub-assistant's system prompt, substituting allowed values into the template.

V6e.name: Set retry and tool-round limits
V6e.blurb: Adds settings for answer-retry attempts and the maximum number of tool-use rounds.

V7a.name: Build error and warning messages
V7a.blurb: Defines how error and warning messages are shaped, formatted, and collected together.

V7b.name: Register message codes
V7b.blurb: Maintains the checked list of all error and warning codes loom can emit.

V7c.name: Fill in message details
V7c.blurb: Renders the variable parts of messages, like values, types, and source text.

V7d.name: Deliver messages to the host
V7d.blurb: Sends batched error and warning messages to the host, with fallback paths.

V8a.name: Cancellation check-in service
V8a.blurb: Provides the swappable service that pauses to check for cancellation at set points.

V8b.name: File access service
V8b.blurb: Provides the swappable service for reading files, including raw bytes and paths.

V8c.name: Data validation service
V8c.blurb: Provides the swappable service that validates data against a schema in one pass.

V8d.name: Time and id services
V8d.blurb: Provides swappable services for the current time, timers, and unique ids.

V8e.name: File-watch and token-count services
V8e.blurb: Provides swappable services for watching files and estimating token counts.

V9a.name: Check the host is compatible
V9a.blurb: Checks required host features exist at start-up and refuses to run if any are missing.

V9b.name: Register the command and watch files
V9b.blurb: Registers loom commands with the host and rebuilds them when files change.

V9c.name: Drive a chat-mode turn
V9c.blurb: Runs a chat-mode question, gets the reply, and limits which tools are active.

V9d.name: Report runtime events to the host
V9d.blurb: Sends runtime status events to the host with consistent display and logging.

V9e.name: Track running commands
V9e.blurb: Keeps a registry of in-flight command runs so they can be managed and cleaned up.

V9f.name: Register tools per command
V9f.blurb: Wires up each command's tools, with friendly labels and clash handling.

V9g.name: Clean up when a session ends
V9g.blurb: Tears down running work in the right order when the host session shuts down.

V9h.name: Handle unknown shutdown reasons
V9h.blurb: Validates the session-end reason and routes unrecognised ones safely.

V9i.name: Run isolated sub-assistants
V9i.blurb: Runs a sub-assistant in its own private session and always disposes it afterward.

V9j.name: Call the model and map provider errors
V9j.blurb: Makes the model call and translates provider errors into typed failures.

V9k.name: Abort start-up on critical failures
V9k.blurb: Stops the whole add-on when a critical start-up registration call fails.

V9l.name: Mark a session needing reload
V9l.blurb: Marks a swapped-in session as degraded and blocks it until reloaded.

V9m.name: Track command-registry state
V9m.blurb: Tracks whether the command registry is live, shutting down, or needs a reload.

V9n.name: Map chat transport errors
V9n.blurb: Turns chat-mode transport failures into typed errors, with cancellation taking priority.

V9o.name: Safely abandon cancelled sub-assistants
V9o.blurb: Ensures a cancelled sub-assistant can't leak a late result into later work.

V9p.name: Survive non-critical start-up failures
V9p.blurb: Keeps running when a non-critical registration call fails, dropping only that piece.

V9q.name: Recover when file-watching stops
V9q.blurb: Leaves a stopped file watcher down and prompts the user to reload.

V10a.name: Find loom files across locations
V10a.blurb: Searches the known places for loom files and resolves naming clashes between them.

V10b.name: Find loom files in packages
V10b.blurb: Locates loom files listed by packages, with glob matching and bounded scanning.

V10c.name: Read and combine settings
V10c.blurb: Reads settings from each source and merges them with project overriding global.

V10d.name: Smooth reloads on file changes
V10d.blurb: Waits out rapid file changes before reloading and serialises rebuilds safely.

V11a.name: Pick the model for filling arguments
V11a.blurb: Resolves which model fills command arguments and checks it supports the needed features.

V11b.name: Feed chat history to argument filling
V11b.blurb: Optionally includes recent conversation so argument-filling has context, rendered compactly.

V11c.name: Shape the argument-filling request
V11c.blurb: Defines the data shape the model must return when filling in command arguments.

V11d.name: Build the argument-filling instructions
V11d.blurb: Builds the instructions that tell the model how to fill in command arguments.

V11e.name: Consistent argument-filling notes
V11e.blurb: Renders short status notes and makes argument-filling repeatable run to run.

V11f.name: Retry argument-filling on failure
V11f.blurb: Retries argument-filling a limited number of times and explains why it failed.

V11g.name: Fill missing arguments with defaults
V11g.blurb: Fills absent arguments with their defaults, then re-checks the whole set is valid.

V11h.name: Show which arguments used defaults
V11h.blurb: Displays the filled-in arguments, marking any that fell back to a default value.

V11i.name: Trim chat history to fit
V11i.blurb: Selects recent whole turns within size limits before handing them to argument-filling.

V11j.name: Cancel argument-filling mid-run
V11j.blurb: Stops the argument-filling model call promptly when the user cancels.

V12a.name: Dispatch and stream a command
V12a.blurb: Runs a slash command, streams the reply, and handles extra typed-in text.

V12b.name: Explain command failures clearly
V12b.blurb: Formats a failed command's error into a readable note tracing where it came from.

V13a.name: Build a question for the model
V13a.blurb: Turns an in-loom question template into the actual text sent to the model.

V13b.name: Infer the answer's expected shape
V13b.blurb: Works out what data shape a typed question expects back from the model.

V13c.name: Let the model use tools while answering
V13c.blurb: Runs the model's tool-use loop and gets a final typed answer back.

V13d.name: Retry malformed model answers
V13d.blurb: Re-asks the model when its answer doesn't fit, up to a set number of times.

V13f.name: Safely abandon cancelled questions
V13f.blurb: Ensures a cancelled model question can't leak a late result into later work.

V13g.name: Log discarded question results
V13g.blurb: Flags when a question's answer is thrown away, keeping any error visible in logs.

V13h.name: Write retry follow-up messages
V13h.blurb: Builds the exact follow-up text sent to the model when re-asking a question.

V14a.name: Call a tool from loom code
V14a.blurb: Lets loom code call a tool directly, with argument checks and clear error types.

V14b.name: Run parallel tool calls together
V14b.blurb: Waits for a batch of simultaneous tool calls to finish before continuing.

V14c.name: Route unusual tool-call outcomes
V14c.blurb: Sends odd tool-call failures to the right channel instead of one generic error.

V14d.name: Handle host-blocked tool calls
V14d.blurb: Surfaces a tool the host refused or errored as a real failure, never a silent success.

V14e.name: Reject too-deeply-nested tool data
V14e.blurb: Blocks tool-call arguments nested past the safety limit before validation.

V14f.name: Safely abandon cancelled tool calls
V14f.blurb: Ensures a cancelled tool call can't leak a late result into later work.

V14g.name: Turn tool results into typed values
V14g.blurb: Converts a tool's raw result into a typed success-or-error value loom code can use.

V15a.name: Call one loom file from another
V15a.blurb: Resolves and loads a called loom file safely, caching parsed results during a run.

V15b.name: Limit and detect call loops
V15b.blurb: Caps how deep loom-to-loom calls can nest and detects circular call chains.

V15c.name: Import shared loom library files
V15c.blurb: Loads reusable library files and reports import and naming problems.

V15d.name: Pause parent during nested chat call
V15d.blurb: Suspends the parent's active tools while a nested chat call runs, then restores them.

V15e.name: Refresh caches on file edits
V15e.blurb: Drops stale cached parses for changed files and everything that imports them.

V15f.name: Report bad cross-file calls
V15f.blurb: Flags wrong arguments, return types, or missing files when calling another loom file.

V15g.name: Track where calls came from
V15g.blurb: Records each call's source file and line so failures can be traced back.

V15h.name: Safely abandon cancelled nested calls
V15h.blurb: Ensures a cancelled nested loom call can't leak a late result into later work.

V15i.name: Control what library files share
V15i.blurb: Defines which library items importers can see and which re-export onward.

V15j.name: Reject too-deeply-nested call data
V15j.blurb: Blocks call arguments or return values nested past the safety limit.

V15k.name: Pass arguments and results between calls
V15k.blurb: Passes arguments and options into a called file and returns its result on success.

V15l.name: Match call style to file type
V15l.blurb: Chooses how a called file runs based on its mode, with its own model and tools.

V15m.name: Cancel nested calls cleanly
V15m.blurb: Adds a cancellation check at call sites and keeps a finished call's effects.

V16a.name: Decide which safety limit wins
V16a.blurb: When several safety limits could fire, picks one to show and notes the rest.

V17a.name: Stop work when cancelled
V17a.blurb: Routes cancel signals through one controller so running work stops promptly.

V17b.name: Handle errors while cancelling
V17b.blurb: Catches faults raised during cancellation without losing the cancellation itself.

V17c.name: Check for cancellation at key points
V17c.blurb: Checks the cancel signal before loops, questions, tool calls, and nested calls.

V18a.name: List required host capabilities
V18a.blurb: Records the host features and surfaces loom depends on, for later verification.

V18b.name: Audit use of host features
V18b.blurb: Checks every host feature the code touches is on the approved inventory list.

V18c.name: Static checks for host upgrades
V18c.blurb: Verifies version pins and host assumptions still hold when upgrading the host.

V18d.name: Run real checks before host upgrade
V18d.blurb: Runs a full example against an upgraded host and reverts the upgrade if it fails.
